attractions.photoSpot: From the north bank of the Thames near St Katharine Docks — 5 min walk.
attractions.tip: Buy online, save £4. Yeoman Warder tours run every 30 min — do not skip.
attractions.didYouKnow: The Tower has housed the Crown Jewels since the 17th century and has served as a royal palace, prison, and mint.
attractions.localSecret: Skip the main gift shop — the independent bookshop at St Katharine Docks has better history titles at half the price.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Perky Blenders Tower Bridge · WatchHouse Tower · Canteen M by Montcalm
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Dishoom Shoreditch · Coppa Club Tower Bridge · Gunpowder
attractions.nearbyTube: Tower Hill (District/Circle) · Tower Gateway (DLR) · London Bridge

attractions.photoSpot: Potters Fields Park (5 min walk) at blue hour.
attractions.tip: Bridge lift times are published online — a bascule lift is unforgettable and free from the pavement.
attractions.didYouKnow: Tower Bridge took eight years to build and opened in 1894 — it is often mistaken for London Bridge.
attractions.localSecret: The glass floor walkway is quieter in the first hour after opening — book the 09:30 slot online.
attractions.nearbyCafes: WatchHouse Tower · Perky Blenders · Horniman at Hays
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Coppa Club · M Restaurant Threadneedle · Brindisa Borough

attractions.photoSpot: From Parliament Square with the abbey's twin west towers.
attractions.tip: Evensong at 17:00 is free and utterly transporting — no camera, just listen.
attractions.didYouKnow: Every British monarch since 1066 has been crowned here, except Edward V and Edward VIII.
attractions.localSecret: The Cellarium Café in the abbey cloisters serves excellent cream tea — most tourists walk past it.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Storey's Gate Café · Westminster Abbey Cellarium · Redemption Roasters
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: The Cinnamon Club · Roux at Parliament Square · Cellarium Café
attractions.nearbyTube: Westminster (exit 6 for Big Ben) · St James's Park

attractions.photoSpot: North end of Westminster Bridge, tripod-friendly late evening.
attractions.tip: Cross to the south bank for the classic composition with the London Eye foreground.
attractions.didYouKnow: Big Ben is actually the name of the bell, not the tower — the tower is Elizabeth Tower.
attractions.localSecret: Locals skip the bridge crowds at sunset and shoot from the Lambeth Pier steps instead — same skyline, half the people.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Storey's Gate Café · Redemption Roasters · Pret Westminster
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Regency Café (8 min) · Poppie's Fish & Chips · Roux at Parliament Square

attractions.photoSpot: From Golden Jubilee Bridge — full wheel + Big Ben in one frame.
attractions.tip: Fast Track ticket saves 60+ min in summer. Sunset flight is worth every pound.
attractions.didYouKnow: Each rotation takes about 30 minutes and on a clear day you can see up to 40 miles.
attractions.localSecret: The free viewing terrace at Oxo Tower (9th floor) gives a similar panorama without the queue — book a cocktail instead.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Monmouth Coffee Southbank · Gabriel's Wharf · FCB Coffee
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Skylon · Oxo Tower Restaurant · Gillray's Steakhouse

attractions.tip: Guard change is Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun in most seasons. Arrive by 10:15 for a spot on the railings.
attractions.didYouKnow: The palace has 775 rooms and the balcony where the Royal Family appears was added in 1850.
attractions.localSecret: Walk through Green Park to The Mall at 10:45 — you'll see the guards marching in without the fence crush.
attractions.nearbyCafes: The Rubens Hotel afternoon tea · Fortnum's at St James's · WatchHouse St James's
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: The Wolseley · Hawksmoor Air Street · Quilon
attractions.nearbyTube: Green Park · Victoria · St James's Park

attractions.photoSpot: Great Court roof from the balcony — Norman Foster's masterpiece.
attractions.tip: Rosetta Stone gets crowded by 11:00. See it first, then work backwards through Egypt and Greece.
attractions.didYouKnow: The museum holds over 8 million objects — less than 1% is on display at any time.
attractions.localSecret: Friday late opening until 20:30 is the quietest time to see the Rosetta Stone without a crowd.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Monmouth Coffee · Store Street Espresso · Great Court Restaurant
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Hawksmoor Seven Dials · Flat Iron · Dishoom Covent Garden
attractions.nearbyTube: Tottenham Court Rd · Holborn · Russell Square

attractions.photoSpot: Hintze Hall with the blue whale from the first floor balcony.
attractions.tip: Use the exhibition-road entrance — no queue, better for prams.
attractions.didYouKnow: Hope the blue whale skeleton replaced Dippy the diplodocus in 2017 and weighs 4.5 tonnes.
attractions.localSecret: Enter via Exhibition Road — the Cromwell Road queue is almost entirely tourists who don't know the side door.
attractions.nearbyCafes: V&A Café · Benugo South Kensington · Fernandez & Wells
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Cacciari's · Bumpkin South Kensington · The Good Earth

attractions.photoSpot: From the Millennium Bridge — Wren's dome dead-centred.
attractions.tip: Climb to the Golden Gallery (528 steps) — the view is worth every one.
attractions.didYouKnow: St Paul's dome was the tallest building in London from 1710 until 1963.
attractions.localSecret: One New Change rooftop (free) gives a stunning eye-level view of the dome — locals use it for proposals.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Pret St Paul's · Costa Paternoster Row · WatchHouse St Paul's
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Bread Street Kitchen · The Restaurant at St Paul's · Caravan City

attractions.photoSpot: Level 72 — the open-air deck. Sunset gold on Tower Bridge below.
attractions.tip: Combined day+night ticket for £10 more — best value in London.
attractions.didYouKnow: At 309.6 metres, The Shard is the tallest building in Western Europe.
attractions.localSecret: Sky Garden is free with advance booking and offers a comparable view — book 3 weeks ahead if The Shard feels pricey.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Monmouth Borough · WatchHouse Shard · FCB London Bridge

attractions.photoSpot: Under the glass roof looking down at street performers.
attractions.tip: Neal's Yard is a 3-minute walk — most tourists never find it.
attractions.didYouKnow: Covent Garden was London's main fruit and vegetable market until 1974 when it moved to Nine Elms.
attractions.localSecret: Walk through Neal's Yard to Monmouth Coffee — skip the piazza chains entirely.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Monmouth Coffee · Flat White · Balthazar Boulangerie
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Dishoom · The Ivy Market Grill · Sushisamba

attractions.photoSpot: Regent's Canal towpath toward the lock at golden hour.
attractions.tip: Walk to Primrose Hill via the canal — 20 min, the best skyline view in London.
attractions.didYouKnow: Camden Lock was built in 1819 and the market grew around the canal trade in the 1970s.
attractions.localSecret: Skip the main street stalls — the Stables Market courtyard has better vintage at lower prices.
attractions.nearbyCafes: Monmouth Coffee Camden · The Coffee Jar · Papersmiths café
attractions.nearbyRestaurants: Hawker House · KERB Camden · Made in Brazil
attractions.nearbyTube: Camden Town · Chalk Farm · Mornington Crescent

stops.desc: Arrive early for Changing of the Guard

stops.transit: Walk · St James's Park · 800 m · 12 min
